rulings.Case Title: SHELLY CARPER et al VS. ADKNOWLEDGE INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Adknowledge, Inc'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Its First Set Of Written Discovery And Request For Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 3, 2014, Line 2, DEFENDANT ADKNOWLEDGE INC. Defendant Adknowledge, Inc'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Its First Set Of Written Discovery And Request For Fees And Costs. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Judge Pro Tem has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's Motion to Compel Further Responses to its First Set of Written Discovery is DENIED. The Plaintiff's electronic service of amended discovery response was effective on July 18, 2014. Defendant's final day to file any motion to compel September 3, 2014. This motion was filed September 9, 2014. Thus, it is untimely. (See Sexton v. Superior Court (1997) 58 Cal.App.4th 1403, 1408-10.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@dlpolaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ANITA VILLARREAL VS. CLEMENT STRESS REDUCTION CENTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Desposition Of Defendant Joy Li You And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants' Counsel Of Record Jay Brown And Mark Dawson In Of $4,560
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 3, 2014, Line 3, PLAINTIFF ANITA VILLARREAL Motion To Compel The Desposition Of Defendant Joy Li You And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants' Counsel Of Record Jay Brown And Mark Dawson In Of $4,560. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Judge Pro Tem has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Deposition of Defendant Joy Li You is GRANTED. Defendant Joy Li You remains a named party in this case. Defendant has articulated no valid grounds for failing or refusing to produce her for deposition. Defendant Joy Li You is ordered to appear for deposition at a mutually agreed upon date within the next fifteen (15) days. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is GRANTED. Defendant is to pay Plaintiff $1,410.00 within thirty (30) days. rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-14-536983, CGC-13-532874 WITH CASE CGC-13-535395
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 3, 2014, Line 7, PLAINTIFF VANCE COOPER's MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-14-536983, CGC-13-532874 WITH CASE CGC-13-535395. rulings.Case Title: THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE CALIFORNIA BY AND THROUGH VS. TAMARA YUROVSKY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motin To Quash Pltff'S Subpoenas Duces Tecum To Airbnb, Inc
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 3, 2014, Line 11, DEFENDANTs TAMARA YUROVSKY, LEV YUROVSKY, TATYANA YUROVSKY, LEV AND TATYANA YUROVSKY, AS TRUSTEES' Motion To Quash Plaintiff's Subpoenas Duces Tecum To Airbnb, Inc. Motion denied. AirBnB is not a "witness" as defined under Cal. Code of Civ. Pro. Section 1985.3. Therefore, Plaintiff was not required to provide a consumer notice prior to serving the subpoenas. Furthermore, the information requested does not impermissibly infringe on Defendants' privacy, financial or otherwise. The Court also finds that the subpoenas do not infringe upon documents protected by the attorney work-product doctrine. Nor does the Court find that the subpoenas are overbroad in time, as the duration of the alleged wrongful conduct is relevant to the determination of a proper civil penalty. (See Cal. Bus. & Prof. Code § 17206(b).) Moreover, Defendants' arguments on First Amendment grounds are moot in light of the agreement between Plaintiff and AirBnB that third parties will not be identified by name in AirBnB's initial production in response to the subpoenas. Additionally, Plaintiff's objections to the documents submitted with the second supplemental declaration are moot because identities of users will not be revealed in AirBnB's initial production. Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted. Finally, Plaintiff's requests for sanctions is granted with modification. Plaintiff is awarded $6,180, based on 30 hours of attorney time. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: LINDSEY E. SCULLY VS. MENLO CAPITAL GROUP, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 3, 2014, Line 9, DEFENDANTs MENLO CAPITAL GROUP, LLC ET AL, MENLO REALTY INCOME PROPERTIES 27, LLC, MENLO REALTY INCOME PROPERTIES 28, LLC, SUNIL SURI, REALTY INCOME CORPORATION's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer as to the First Cause of Action for breach of contract is sustained in part and overruled in part. The demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to inclusion of the proper parties to the contract under the alter ego doctrine. Plaintiff must allege, in good faith, "sufficient facts to show a unity of interest and ownership, and an unjust result if the corporation is treated as the sole actor." Leek v. Cooper (2011) 194 Cal.App.4th 399, 415. The demurrer as to the First Cause of Action is overruled as to whether the language of the Agreement encompasses the named parties and whether breach has occurred. The demurrer as to the Second Cause of Action for Unjust Enrichment should be sustained in part and overruled in part. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to Defendant Menlo Capital Group LLC because it is undisputed that Defendant Menlo Capital Group LLC is a party to the Agreement and the Plaintiff cannot pursue a breach of contract cause of action and unjust enrichment cause of action simultaneously. Wal-Noon Corp. v. Hill, (1975) 45 Cal.App.3d 605, 613. The demurrer is overruled as to Defendants Sunil Suri, Menlo Realty Income Properties 27, LLC, Menlo Realty Income Properties 28, LLC, and Realty Income Corporation because the language of the Agreement may or may not encompass these defendants and the Plaintiff is allowed to allege multiple and conflicting theories of recovery. Flournoy v. State, (1969) 275 Cal.App.2d 806, 811. The demurrer as to the Third Cause of Action for Fraud is sustained is sustained without leave to amend under the economic loss rule because it is not conceptually distinct from the Agreement. See United Guaranty Mortgage Indemnity Co. v. Countrywide Fin. Corp. (C.D. Cal. 2009) 660 F.Supp2d 1163, 1184. The demurrer as to the Fourth Cause of Action for breach of the implied covenant is overruled. The demurrer as to the Sixth Cause of Action for violations of Business & Professions Code § 17200 et seq. is overruled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
